title: ranunculus pantothrix var. peucedanifolius
side_effects: Ranunculus pantothrix var. peucedanifolius can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues. Ingesting the plant's toxic compounds can cause symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, while prolonged exposure can lead to more severe complications. There is also a risk of liver and kidney damage if the plant's toxins accumulate in the body over time. Improper use of Ranunculus pantothrix var. peucedanifolius can also exacerbate pre-existing conditions, such as diabetes and epilepsy.
